title: Regression: 'podman invalid-command' exits 0, no longer outputs to stderr
body:
podman-0.8.5-1.gitdc5a711.fc28, when invoked with an invalid subcommand, no longer errors:
```
# podman not-a-command
Command "not-a-command" not found.            <<< this goes to stdout, not stderr
See `podman --help`.
# echo $?
0
```
This is a regression from podman-0.8.4-2.git9f9b8cf.fc28:
```
# podman not-a-command
No help topic for 'not-a-command'         <<< on stderr
# echo $?
3
```

comments.body: @edsantiago, thanks for the report! I will fix that.
@rhatdan @edsantiago Docker has exit code 1 in such case. Shall we follow that or shall we keep 3 as before?

I think 1 is better here

On Sat, Sep 1, 2018, 13:59 Valentin Rothberg <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> @rhatdan <https://github.com/rhatdan> @edsantiago
> <https://github.com/edsantiago> Docker has exit code 1 in such case.
> Shall we follow that or shall we keep 3 as before?
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1395#issuecomment-417876909>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AHYHCDArbhX3FUAm6Dkb6hwaQxkWe9fbks5uWsrwgaJpZM4WWS1B>
> .
>

TBH I was surprised at seeing 3 in the earlier podman: I tend to think of exit codes as 0 and 1, with everything else reserved for special, clearly-documented situations. In short: 1 seems more natural.
I agree, getting 3 is rather exotic. PR #1397 fixes the issue.
